This essential work helps to provide a reliable electricity supply to your property, reducing the risk of any unplanned power cuts in the future. To keep everyone safe while we carry out these works, we will need to temporarily turn off your electricity. Steps.Message:
Sent by Will at 02:26 - I'm pleased to confirm that all power in the Orford Street area should be back on. The issue was caused by an unexpected underground cable fault, which our engineers have diverted power away from.

We know that a power cut can be difficult, and I'm sorry for the length of the time you were without your electricity. Please rest assured that our staff will continue to work around the clock to keep the lights on.

If your power isn't on or there's anything you'd like to talk to us about, call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:

We're sorry we've had to change the estimated time, but we'll keep you updated.

I'll let you know once our teams arrive.

Sent by Talie at 12:36 - Thanks for your patience today. Our engineers have now investigated the reports of overhead cabling on the ground, and have deemed it safe to restore power back to all 403 properties in the Sandy, SG19 3 area.

We're very sorry we had to turn your power off unexpectedly, and with so little warning. We usually provide much more notice if we can, but in a situation like this our first priority is the safety of the public and our staff.

Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Talie at 12:09 - We're very sorry we've had to switch off your electricity. Our engineers have turned off the power in the Sandy, SG19 3 area in the interest of the public's health and safety. However, it was only necessary to interrupt power to some properties for a short while, so you might find your power only went off briefly. This is so they can investigate reports of an overhead cable that is on the ground. 

If in the area, please take care and stay at least 10m away from the cable. 

Until our engineers arrive on site and investigate, it's difficult to advise you a time frame. However, based on an average, our estimated time to have you back on is between 13:30 and 14:30.
